StdInvariant.FuzzSelector[]","name":"targetedSelectors_","type":"tuple[]"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"targetSenders","outputs":[{"internalType":"address[]","name":"targetedSenders_","type":"address[]"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"We inline all math from the actual contracts to keep Halmos in pure-only mode      (no external calls, no storage). All `check_*` functions are formal verification targets. ## Architecture Under Test   Bridge (LRC20B)           Vault (LiquidLUX)         Fee Distribution           Teleport   +------------+           +------------------+       +--------------+          +----------+   | bridgeMint |--deposit->| _convertToShares |--fee->| receiveFees  |--lock--->| lock src |   | bridgeBurn |<-withdraw-| _convertToAssets |       | perfFee+net  |          | mint dst |   +------------+           +------------------+       +--------------+          +----------+ ## Solver Strategy   Uses typed inputs to constrain bitvector width for SMT tractability.   All assertions are carefully structured to use only linear arithmetic or   at most one nonlinear product per assertion, avoiding cross-multiplication   of symbolic variables against large constants (BPS=10000, VIRTUAL=1e6).   Where nonlinear reasoning is required (vault share math), we use uint8 with   scaled-down constants (VIRTUAL=10, MIN_LIQ=10) and prove the floor-division   property: floor(a*b/c)*c <= a*b. This algebraic identity holds at any bit width.   For fee/router math (division by BPS=10000), we decompose proofs into   sub-properties that involve only one floor() per assertion, matching the   decomposition approach from HalmosAMM.t.sol. ## Invariants Proven (23 check_ functions)   BRIDGE (3):       conservation, multi-step, daily limit   DEPOSIT (3):      share proportionality, first-depositor roundtrip, second-depositor roundtrip   TELEPORT (2):     two-chain conservation, multi-hop 3-chain   SELF-REPAY (1):   debt floor   SOLVENCY (1):     withdrawal preserves collateralization   YIELD (2):        full roundtrip, partial deallocate   FEES (4):         accounting identity, full accounting, treasury cap, exchange rate monotonicity   ROUTER (2):       3-recipient conservation, equal-weight symmetry   COLLECT (3):      accounting identity, multi-cycle, full pipeline   E2E (2):          cross-chain total supply, full lifecycle","kind":"dev","methods":{"excludeArtifacts()":{"details":"Returns artifact identifiers configured via `excludeArtifact`."},"excludeContracts()":{"details":"Returns contract addresses configured via `excludeContract`."},"excludeSelectors()":{"details":"Returns selector exclusions configured via `excludeSelector`."},"excludeSenders()":{"details":"Returns senders configured via `excludeSender`."},"targetArtifactSelectors()":{"details":"Returns artifact-selector targets configured via `targetArtifactSelector`."},"targetArtifacts()":{"details":"Returns artifact identifiers configured via `targetArtifact`."},"targetContracts()":{"details":"Returns contract addresses configured via `targetContract`."},"targetInterfaces()":{"details":"Returns address-interface targets configured via `targetInterface`."},"targetSelectors()":{"details":"Returns selector targets configured via `targetSelector`."},"targetSenders()":{"details":"Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist)."}},"title":"HalmosE2ETest - End-to-end symbolic proofs for L* token lifecycle","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"check_bridgeConservation(uint32,uint32)":{"notice":"Prove: mint/burn conservation identity"},"check_bridgeDailyLimitEnforcement(uint32,uint32,uint32,uint32)":{"notice":"Prove: minted-in-period never exceeds dailyMintLimit"},"check_bridgeMultiStepConservation(uint24,uint24,uint24,uint24)":{"notice":"Prove: sequential mint/burn ops maintain conservation"},"check_collectAccountingIdentity(uint32,uint32,uint32)":{"notice":"Prove: Collect: total == pending + bridged"},"check_collectMultipleBridgeCycles(uint24,uint24,uint24,uint24)":{"notice":"Prove: identity holds through multiple push/bridge cycles"},"check_collectVaultRouterPipeline(uint32,uint16,uint16)":{"notice":"Prove: Collect->Vault->Router pipeline has zero value leak We decompose: (1) Collect accounting is exact (linear). (2) Router distribution is bounded (proven by check_routerDistributionConservation). (3) Composition: vault receives exactly what Collect bridges, Router distributes <= vault balance."},"check_crossChainTotalSupply(uint24,uint24,uint24,uint24,uint24)":{"notice":"Prove: cross-chain total supply conserved through mint+teleport+burn"},"check_depositShareProportionality(uint8,uint8,uint8)":{"notice":"Prove: depositor gets shares with vault-favorable rounding"},"check_depositWithdrawRoundTrip(uint8)":{"notice":"Prove: deposit then immediate withdraw returns <= deposited"},"check_exchangeRateMonotonicWithFees(uint32,uint32,uint32)":{"notice":"Prove: exchange rate is non-decreasing after fee injection rate = (assets + V) / (supply + V). Adding to numerator with fixed denominator can only increase the quotient. We prove this via the algebraic identity: (a + x) / d >= a / d  when x >= 0 and d > 0. Decomposed: floor((a+x)*M/d) >= floor(a*M/d) follows from (a+x)*M >= a*M."},"check_feeDistributionAccounting(uint32,uint16)":{"notice":"Prove: perfFee + vaultNet == amount (zero value leak)"},"check_feeDistributionFullAccounting(uint8,uint8,uint8)":{"notice":"Prove: full accounting identity with slashing reserve Decomposed proof: (1) fee+net==amt is linear. (2) net >= reserve reduces to fee <= amt - reserve, which holds when bps_fee + bps_slash <= BPS. We prove each sub-property independently."},"check_feeDistributionTreasuryCap(uint32,uint16)":{"notice":"Prove: treasury perfFee <= amount (fee never exceeds input) We prove fee <= amt directly. Since feeBps <= 2000 and BPS == 10000, fee = floor(amt * feeBps / 10000) <= floor(amt * 2000 / 10000) = floor(amt/5) <= amt. The floor division property floor(a*b/c) <= a when b <= c is proven via:   floor(a*b/c) * c <= a*b <= a*c  (when b <= c)   => floor(a*b/c) <= a"},"check_fullE2ELifecycle(uint8,uint8,uint8)":{"notice":"Prove: full bridge->deposit->yield->withdraw->burn lifecycle is sound Uses uint8 with scaled constants for vault math tractability. Fee calculation uses BPS=10000 but only linear assertions."},"check_routerDistributionConservation(uint32,uint16,uint16)":{"notice":"Prove: 3-recipient weighted distribution never exceeds input, dust <= 2 Each share_i = floor(amount * w_i / BASE). Since floor(x) <= x, sum(share_i) = sum(floor(amt*w_i/B)) <= sum(amt*w_i/B) = amt*sum(w_i)/B = amt. Dust = amt - sum <= 2 (each of 3 floor operations loses at most 1)."},"check_routerEqualWeights(uint32)":{"notice":"Prove: equal weights give equal shares"},"check_secondDepositorRoundTrip(uint8,uint8)":{"notice":"Prove: second depositor cannot profit from deposit-then-withdraw"},"check_selfRepayFloor(uint32,uint32)":{"notice":"Prove: self-repay never causes debt underflow or over-repayment"},"check_teleportConservation(uint32,uint32,uint32)":{"notice":"Prove: teleport preserves combined supply across two chains"},"check_teleportMultiHop(uint24,uint24,uint24,uint24,uint24)":{"notice":"Prove: two-hop teleport preserves total across 3 chains"},"check_withdrawalSolvency(uint32,uint32,uint32,uint32)":{"notice":"Prove: withdrawal preserves 200% collateralization"},"check_yieldPartialDeallocate(uint24,uint24,uint24,uint24)":{"notice":"Prove: partial deallocate preserves vault + strategy total"},"check_yieldStrategyConservation(uint32,uint32,uint24)":{"notice":"Prove: allocate->earn->deallocate = original + yield"}},"notice":"Proves safety invariants across: bridge mint/burn -> LiquidLUX deposit -> yield/fees -> targetSenders().details: Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist).

check_bridgeMultiStepConservation(uint24.notice: Prove: sequential mint/burn ops maintain conservation

check_collectMultipleBridgeCycles(uint24.notice: Prove: identity holds through multiple push/bridge cycles

check_collectVaultRouterPipeline(uint32,.notice: Prove: Collect->Vault->Router pipeline has zero value leak We decompose: (1) Collect accounting is exact (linear). (2) Router distribution is bounded (proven by check_routerDistributionConservation). (3) Composition: vault receives exactly what Collect bridges, Router distributes <= vault balance.

check_crossChainTotalSupply(uint24,uint2.notice: Prove: cross-chain total supply conserved through mint+teleport+burn

check_depositShareProportionality(uint8,.notice: Prove: depositor gets shares with vault-favorable rounding

check_depositWithdrawRoundTrip(uint8).notice: Prove: deposit then immediate withdraw returns <= deposited

check_exchangeRateMonotonicWithFees(uint.notice: Prove: exchange rate is non-decreasing after fee injection rate = (assets + V) / (supply + V). Adding to numerator with fixed denominator can only increase the quotient. We prove this via the algebraic identity: (a + x) / d >= a / d  when x >= 0 and d > 0. Decomposed: floor((a+x)*M/d) >= floor(a*M/d) follows from (a+x)*M >= a*M.

check_feeDistributionAccounting(uint32,u.notice: Prove: perfFee + vaultNet == amount (zero value leak)

check_feeDistributionFullAccounting(uint.notice: Prove: full accounting identity with slashing reserve Decomposed proof: (1) fee+net==amt is linear. (2) net >= reserve reduces to fee <= amt - reserve, which holds when bps_fee + bps_slash <= BPS. We prove each sub-property independently.

check_feeDistributionTreasuryCap(uint32,.notice: Prove: treasury perfFee <= amount (fee never exceeds input) We prove fee <= amt directly. Since feeBps <= 2000 and BPS == 10000, fee = floor(amt * feeBps / 10000) <= floor(amt * 2000 / 10000) = floor(amt/5) <= amt. The floor division property floor(a*b/c) <= a when b <= c is proven via:   floor(a*b/c) * c <= a*b <= a*c  (when b <= c)   => floor(a*b/c) <= a

check_fullE2ELifecycle(uint8,uint8,uint8.notice: Prove: full bridge->deposit->yield->withdraw->burn lifecycle is sound Uses uint8 with scaled constants for vault math tractability. Fee calculation uses BPS=10000 but only linear assertions.

check_routerDistributionConservation(uin.notice: Prove: 3-recipient weighted distribution never exceeds input, dust <= 2 Each share_i = floor(amount * w_i / BASE). Since floor(x) <= x, sum(share_i) = sum(floor(amt*w_i/B)) <= sum(amt*w_i/B) = amt*sum(w_i)/B = amt. Dust = amt - sum <= 2 (each of 3 floor operations loses at most 1).

check_routerEqualWeights(uint32).notice: Prove: equal weights give equal shares

check_secondDepositorRoundTrip(uint8,uin.notice: Prove: second depositor cannot profit from deposit-then-withdraw

check_selfRepayFloor(uint32,uint32).notice: Prove: self-repay never causes debt underflow or over-repayment

check_teleportConservation(uint32,uint32.notice: Prove: teleport preserves combined supply across two chains

check_teleportMultiHop(uint24,uint24,uin.notice: Prove: two-hop teleport preserves total across 3 chains

check_yieldPartialDeallocate(uint24,uint.notice: Prove: partial deallocate preserves vault + strategy total

@notice Prove: full accounting identity with slashing reserve
 Decomposed proof: (1) fee+net==amt is linear. (2) net >= reserve
 reduces to fee <= amt - reserve, which holds when bps_fee + bps_slash <= BPS.
 We prove each sub-property independently.

@notice Prove: treasury perfFee <= amount (fee never exceeds input)
 We prove fee <= amt directly. Since feeBps <= 2000 and BPS == 10000,
 fee = floor(amt * feeBps / 10000) <= floor(amt * 2000 / 10000) = floor(amt/5) <= amt.
 The floor division property floor(a*b/c) <= a when b <= c is proven via:
   floor(a*b/c) * c <= a*b <= a*c  (when b <= c)
   => floor(a*b/c) <= a

@notice Prove: exchange rate is non-decreasing after fee injection
 rate = (assets + V) / (supply + V). Adding to numerator with fixed denominator
 can only increase the quotient. We prove this via the algebraic identity:
 (a + x) / d >= a / d  when x >= 0 and d > 0.
 Decomposed: floor((a+x)*M/d) >= floor(a*M/d) follows from (a+x)*M >= a*M.

@notice Prove: 3-recipient weighted distribution never exceeds input, dust <= 2
 Each share_i = floor(amount * w_i / BASE). Since floor(x) <= x,
 sum(share_i) = sum(floor(amt*w_i/B)) <= sum(amt*w_i/B) = amt*sum(w_i)/B = amt.
 Dust = amt - sum <= 2 (each of 3 floor operations loses at most 1).
@notice Prove: equal weights give equal shares
@notice Prove: Collect: total == pending + bridged
@notice Prove: identity holds through multiple push/bridge cycles

@notice Prove: Collect->Vault->Router pipeline has zero value leak
 We decompose: (1) Collect accounting is exact (linear). (2) Router distribution
 is bounded (proven by check_routerDistributionConservation). (3) Composition:
 vault receives exactly what Collect bridges, Router distributes <= vault balance.
@notice Prove: cross-chain total supply conserved through mint+teleport+burn

@title HalmosE2ETest - End-to-end symbolic proofs for L* token lifecycle
 @notice Proves safety invariants across: bridge mint/burn -> LiquidLUX deposit -> yield/fees -> teleport
 @dev We inline all math from the actual contracts to keep Halmos in pure-only mode
      (no external calls, no storage). All `check_*` functions are formal verification targets.
 ## Architecture Under Test
   Bridge (LRC20B)           Vault (LiquidLUX)         Fee Distribution           Teleport
   +------------+           +------------------+       +--------------+          +----------+
   | bridgeMint |--deposit->| _convertToShares |--fee->| receiveFees  |--lock--->| lock src |
   | bridgeBurn |<-withdraw-| _convertToAssets |       | perfFee+net  |          | mint dst |
   +------------+           +------------------+       +--------------+          +----------+
 ## Solver Strategy
   Uses typed inputs to constrain bitvector width for SMT tractability.
   All assertions are carefully structured to use only linear arithmetic or
   at most one nonlinear product per assertion, avoiding cross-multiplication
   of symbolic variables against large constants (BPS=10000, VIRTUAL=1e6).
   Where nonlinear reasoning is required (vault share math), we use uint8 with
   scaled-down constants (VIRTUAL=10, MIN_LIQ=10) and prove the floor-division
   property: floor(a*b/c)*c <= a*b. This algebraic identity holds at any bit width.
   For fee/router math (division by BPS=10000), we decompose proofs into
   sub-properties that involve only one floor() per assertion, matching the
   decomposition approach from HalmosAMM.t.sol.
 ## Invariants Proven (23 check_ functions)
   BRIDGE (3):       conservation, multi-step, daily limit
   DEPOSIT (3):      share proportionality, first-depositor roundtrip, second-depositor roundtrip
   TELEPORT (2):     two-chain conservation, multi-hop 3-chain
   SELF-REPAY (1):   debt floor
   SOLVENCY (1):     withdrawal preserves collateralization
   YIELD (2):        full roundtrip, partial deallocate
   FEES (4):         accounting identity, full accounting, treasury cap, exchange rate monotonicity
   ROUTER (2):       3-recipient conservation, equal-weight symmetry
   COLLECT (3):      accounting identity, multi-cycle, full pipeline
   E2E (2):          cross-chain total supply, full lifecycle
